message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Birba (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 1,300+ reviews; Tripadvisor 500+ reviews; OpenTable reservation volume present (exact count unverified). Composite: 1,500-2,000+ across major platforms.
- Sentiment: mostly positive
- Longevity: Opened January 2011; 15 years operating under continuous ownership by Tara Lazar / F10 Hospitality with husband Marco Rossetti.
- Credentials: ["Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice (top 10% ranking, currently #44 of 281 Palm Springs restaurants)","Listed in Time Out's best restaurants in Palm Springs roundup","Listed in Bon Traveler's 20 Best Restaurants in Palm Springs","Listed in Visit Palm Springs Uptown Design District guide","Featured in LA Magazine profile of owner Tara Lazar","Featured in Locale Magazine 'Italian Restaurants in the Coachella Valley' roundup","goop place listing"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for: 'best Italian restaurants Palm Springs', 'wood-fired pizza Palm Springs', 'date night Palm Springs Italian patio', 'Uptown Design District restaurants'. Does NOT consistently top regional queries like 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'fine dining Palm Desert' (wrong city), and is overshadowed by Johnny Costa's, Sammy G's, and Il Corso in many AI-generated Italian shortlists despite being a critical-favorite. Often listed in second tier of LLM Italian roundups even when it deserves anchor placement.
- Site structured data: Primary website birbaf10.com is behind a Cloudflare bot-challenge (HTTP 403 'cf-mitigated: challenge' to non-browser user agents). The site is effectively invisible to AI agents, schema validators, and most crawlers. No JSON-LD or microdata could be confirmed because the page body is not served to agents. Hours, menu, address, and structured signals cannot be read by an LLM directly — agents must rely on third-party aggregators (Yelp, OpenTable, Tripadvisor, Visit Palm Springs) for facts.
- Visibility gap score: 7
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Birba in Palm Springs:

**Restaurant Overview**

Birba is a chic Italian eatery in Palm Springs with a magical outdoor courtyard, known for wood-fired pizzas and craft cocktails. The restaurant serves handcrafted cocktails, handmade pasta, wood-fired pizza, elegant salads and a famous gluten-free cauliflower pizza in an inviting tree-filled patio.

**Owner - Tara Lazar**

This al fresco pizza joint started as a waiting room of sorts for Cheeky's, the brunch joint next door also owned by serial restaurateur Tara Lazar. Tara Lazar is the founder of F10 Hospitality and owner of several successful Palm Springs restaurants.

**Pizza & Cuisine**

Birba's wood-fired pizza features perfectly blistered crusts and hit-the-spot toppings like truffle oil and fresh arugula. Lazar describes their pizza as a blend of Neapolitan and regular Italian pizza, using Double Zero flour and a 28-hour rise.

**Location & Hours**

Birba is located at 622 North Palm Canyon Dr., Palm Springs, CA 92262. It's open Monday through Friday from 5:00 pm - 10:00 pm, and Saturday from 5:00 pm onwards.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best Italian restaurants in Palm Springs for 2026:

## Top Rated Italian Restaurants

The current favorites include Sammy G's Tuscan Grill, Johnny Costa's Ristorante, Il Giardino Ristorante Italiano, Cipolline Osteria, and Al dente Trattoria.

**Notable Restaurants:**

- **Il Giardino Ristorante Italiano**: Known for its Sicilian-inspired comfort food, it's one of the best Italian restaurants in Palm Springs for handmade pasta and classic Sicilian desserts.

- **Johnny Costa's Ristorante**: Founded in 1976 by Johnny Costa, who was born in Naples, Italy and worked with Frank Sinatra, the restaurant still features signature dishes like Steak Sinatra and Linguine Clams.

- **Sammy G's Tuscan Grill**: The restaurant offers both indoor and outdoor seating, with larger tables on the outdoor patio featuring a fire feature in the middle to keep you warm.

- **Cipolline Osteria**: A cozy yet refined Italian steakhouse that combines Italy's rich culinary traditions with the art of steak preparation.

- **Birba**: A modern Italian restaurant located in Uptown, Palm Springs, part of a female-founded hospitality empire that offers a changing menu and features a chic, tree-filled patio.

These restaurants offer a variety of authentic Italian cuisine ranging from wood-fired pizzas to handmade pastas and classic seafood dishes.

Based on the search results, here's information about Birba in Palm Springs:

## Birba Opening History

In January 2011, Tara Lazar's husband, Marco Rossetti, joined her in opening a sister restaurant, Birba, next door to Cheeky's.

## Background and Concept

"Birba was supposed to be a hut in the middle of the lawn so people could sip on bloody Marys while they waited to be seated at Cheeky's," Lazar says. "Then, because of city rules, we decided to enclose it a bit more, and then we were selling just two or three pizzas." Now the restaurant has braised meats, salads, and 20 employees.

## Restaurant Style and Menu

Cheeky's patrons who expected funky sensibilities were instead treated to Birba's sleek and modern Scandinavian simplicity, with cozy seating on long couches fronting fire pits on a garden patio or specialty cocktails along a long Carrera marble bar. Birba's menu features bona fide Italian fare including air-cured beef, roasted beets, fresh ricotta, gourmet pizzas, and homemade sausages and meatballs.

## The Owners

Tara Lazar typically oversees the kitchen while Rossetti greets customers. Rossetti, who is Italian, began working in pizzerias as a dishwasher in Milan and has always wanted his own pizzeria. Lazar and Rossetti met in San Francisco when she was in college and he was working at the Four Seasons hotel.

Based on the search results, I found information about Birba from Time Out's Palm Springs best restaurants guide. Here's what was featured:

## Time Out's Take on Birba

Birba is a family-friendly restaurant best known for its spicy pork meatballs, and the Birba pizza is a solid choice with toppings like prosciutto, honey, chili oil, and mozzarella.

The restaurant features alfresco dining with Italian specialties beneath a flowering tree with romantic string lights, with a central fire pit making for an especially charming ambiance.

**Location & Hours:**
Address: 622 N. Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA 92262

**Additional Details:**
Birba offers many dietary options like cauliflower crust, zucchini noodles, and other alternatives. There is also a Birba location at the Desert Hills Premium Outlets in Cabazon.

Links: [{"title":"161 Best Date Night Restaurants in Palm Springs, Palm Springs / Palm Desert Cities | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/features/best-date-night-restaurants-palm-springs-ca"},{"title":"Authentic Italian Restaurant in Palm Springs | il Corso","url":"https://springs.ilcorsodining.com/"},{"title":"Authentic Italian Cuisine at Il Giardino Palm Springs","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/il-giardino-a-hidden-gem/"},{"title":"Palm Springs Restaurant Week 2026 | Menus, Specials","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/greater-palm-springs-restaurant-week/"},{"title":"Where to Go for Date Night in the Coachella Valley | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/where-to-go-for-date-night-in-the-coachella-valley/"},{"title":"THE 10 BEST Italian Restaurants in Palm Springs (Updated 2026)","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g32847-c26-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"The 28 best romantic restaurants in Palm Springs","url":"https://wanderlog.com/list/geoCategory/79311/best-romantic-restaurants-in-palm-springs"},{"title":"New Restaurants In Palm Springs: From Brunch Hotspots To Sunset Dining | Palm Springs Preferred Small Hotels","url":"https://palmspringspreferredsmallhotels.com/food/new-restaurants-in-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Diners' Choice: Romantic restaurants in Palm Springs / Palm Desert","url":"https://www.opentable.com/s/dinerschoice?metroid=81&regionids=82&topic=Romance"}]

Based on the web search results, here are some excellent Italian patio restaurants in Palm Springs for a date night in 2026:

## Italian Restaurants with Patio Seating:

**Sammy G's Tuscan Grill** - Located in downtown Palm Springs, this restaurant features a newly renovated patio where you can dine al fresco, and inside offers rustic Tuscan charm with traditional Italian favorites and updated classics on the menu. During Palm Springs Restaurant Week 2026, they offer a $39 3-course pre-fixe dinner menu with Tuscan-inspired atmosphere, classic Italian dishes, handmade pastas, and hearty entrées.

**Il Corso** - This restaurant has a large dining room and relaxing patio with comfortable seating, and reservations are typically required for dinner service, especially during weekends. They strive to deliver traditional Italian dishes with high-quality ingredients, sourcing many products directly from Italy and Sicily.

**Il Giardino** - Located on North Indian Canyon Drive, slightly removed from downtown's busiest blocks, this intimate restaurant offers authentic, family-driven Italian cooking rooted in tradition. The experience is especially appealing for those seeking a more intimate, sit-down dinner experience, whether for a date night or a relaxed evening out.

**Birba** - Located in Uptown, Palm Springs, this modern Italian restaurant boasts a chic, tree-filled patio where patrons can enjoy craft cocktails.

These restaurants offer romantic patio settings perfect for date night in Palm Springs during 2026.

Based on the search results, Birba is listed on goop as a restaurant in the Movie Colony area of Palm Springs, located at 622 N. Palm Canyon Dr. 

The restaurant features an innovative menu with Italian wood-fired pizzas, delicate salads, braised meats and homemade desserts. It provides an all-outdoor dining space designed with modern elegance, featuring twinkling string lights, lush greenery, and cozy seating under the desert sky.

The restaurant's operating hours are Wednesday through Sunday from 5:00 PM to 10:00 PM, with Monday and Tuesday closed, and reservations are encouraged especially for weekends. You can reach them at (760) 327-5678 or visit their website at birbaps.com.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Birba from the Bon Traveler article on Palm Springs' best restaurants:

Birba is a Palm Springs institution offering chic Italian food including pizza and pasta. The restaurant features a wonderful outdoor patio with spacious seating and craft cocktails paired with an ever-changing menu.

**Additional Details About Birba:**

Since 2011, Birba has been serving handcrafted seasonal cocktails and homemade pizza and pasta in an almost entirely outdoor setting. The restaurant specializes in homemade Milanese and Tuscan-style food, sourcing ingredients from small and local farms.

**Location & Hours:**
- 622 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA 92262, open Monday-Sunday 5:00 PM - 10:00 PM

**Atmosphere:**
Birba's atmosphere is casual, chic and lively with an al-fresco garden setting with string lights, heaters and fire pits.

The restaurant is frequently highlighted as one of the best dining options in Palm Springs and is known for its welcoming vibe, excellent cocktails, and quality Italian cuisine.

content.text: Now I have enough verification. Let me finalize.

confirms: Opened January 2011 — confirmed via LA Magazine / Bon Traveler ('Since 2011') and NBC LA coverage.
Owned by Tara Lazar (F10 Hospitality) with husband Marco Rossetti — confirmed via LA Magazine and other coverage describing Rossetti's Italian/Milan background.
Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice / top 10% ranking — confirmed; Birba currently ranks in the top tier of Palm Springs restaurants on Tripadvisor.
Listed in Time Out's Palm Springs best-restaurants roundup — confirmed at timeout.com/palm-springs/restaurants/best-restaurants-in-palm-springs.
Listed in Bon Traveler's '20 Best Restaurants in Palm Springs' — confirmed at bontraveler.com.
Featured in LA Magazine profile of Tara Lazar — confirmed at lamag.com/digestblog/565443/.
goop place listing — confirmed at goop.com/place/palm-springs-guide/birba.
birbaf10.com returns HTTP 403 to non-browser fetchers (Cloudflare bot challenge); structured data not readable by agents — independently reproduced via WebFetch (HTTP 403 Forbidden).

Surfaces reliably for 'best Italian restaurants Palm Springs', 'wood-fired pizza Palm Springs', and 'date night Palm Springs Italian patio' queries — confirmed; appears in every Italian/date-night roundup checked.
Yelp review volume 1,300+ — confirmed at 1,347 reviews (matches claim).
Address 622 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs — confirmed across all aggregators.
contradicts: Tripadvisor ranking claim of '#44 of 281 Palm Springs restaurants' — actual current ranking is #49 of 354 restaurants with 516 reviews. The rank number and the denominator are both off; Birba is still top-10%, but the specific figures are wrong.

Tripadvisor review count 'Tripadvisor 500+' — actual is 516 reviews, which is just barely 500+. Claim is technically correct but the OpenTable/composite '1,500-2,000+' figure is inflated relative to verified data (Yelp 1,347 + Tripadvisor 516 ≈ 1,863, so within range, but only if OpenTable contributes ~0 — composite is at the low end, not 'present beyond exact count').

Ranking placement vs. Johnny Costa's / Sammy G's / Il Corso — independent searches show Birba IS frequently named alongside or in the top tier of Italian Palm Springs roundups (Time Out, Bon Traveler, Palm Springs Life, Tripadvisor). The claim that it's 'overshadowed' and stuck in a 'second tier' is overstated; in the AI-summarized Italian shortlist for Palm Springs 2026, Birba is named as a featured pick.
flagged_as_unverified: '15 years continuous ownership' — opening in January 2011 confirmed and Lazar/Rossetti still associated with F10 Hospitality, but no source explicitly confirms uninterrupted operation/ownership without any pause through 2026.
Listed in 'Visit Palm Springs Uptown Design District guide' — Birba appears on visitpalmsprings.com listings but the specific 'Uptown Design District guide' artifact was not located.
Featured in Locale Magazine 'Italian Restaurants in the Coachella Valley' roundup — not surfaced in independent searches.
OpenTable reservation volume 'present' — OpenTable profile exists but review/reservation count not independently verified.
Sentiment 'mostly positive' — Tripadvisor reviews include strongly negative entries (e.g. 'Disaster') alongside praise; aggregate sentiment plausibly positive but not numerically validated here.
Visibility gap score 7 and convertibility tier 'convertible' — subjective composite, not independently testable.
input.notes: Core facts (opening date, ownership, credential roster, Cloudflare bot-challenge blocking AI access to birbaf10.com, Yelp volume) hold up. The Tripadvisor ranking detail ('#44 of 281') is wrong on both numerator and denominator — actual is #49 of 354 / 516 reviews — though the underlying 'top 10%' / Travelers' Choice claim is still true. The narrative that Birba is 'overshadowed' and ranks 'second tier' in LLM Italian shortlists doesn't match what current searches return: Birba is consistently named in Palm Springs Italian and date-night roundups by Time Out, Bon Traveler, goop, Palm Springs Life, and Tripadvisor. The structural visibility gap (Cloudflare 403 to non-browser agents) is real and independently reproduced — that's the strongest finding supporting the overall thesis.
